485	\begin{lemma}[The relative tensor of modules is a coequalizer of abelian groups]
486	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer}
487	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork,
488	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actN,
489	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actM,
490	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap,
491	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap,
492	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL,
493	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_surjective,
494	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_comp_act,
495	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aL,
496	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aR,
497	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor,
498	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor_epi,
499	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition,
500	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.cofork,
501	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descHom,
502	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descMor,
503	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descFac,
504	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul,
505	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap_tmul,
506	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL_tmul,
507	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.piMor_apply,
508	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.descHom_tmul}
509	  \uses{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib}
510	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(M, N\) be \(S\)-modules. The abelian group
511	  \(M \otimes_S N\) is the coequalizer, \emph{in the category
512	  \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of abelian groups}, of the two parallel
513	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-linear \(S\)-action maps
514	  \[
515	    a_L,\, a_R :
516	    M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} (S \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N)
517	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
518	    M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N,
519	  \]
520	  where the \emph{left} action map sends \(m \otimes (s \otimes n) \mapsto
521	  (s \cdot m) \otimes n\) and the \emph{right} action map sends
522	  \(m \otimes (s \otimes n) \mapsto m \otimes (s \cdot n)\). The coforking map is
523	  the canonical projection \(\pi : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to M \otimes_S N\),
524	  \(m \otimes n \mapsto m \otimes_S n\).
525	\end{lemma}
526	
527	\begin{proof}
528	  \uses{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib}
529	  Write \(a_N : S \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to N\) and
530	  \(a_M : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} S \to M\) for the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-linear maps
531	  induced by the scalar action (\(s \otimes n \mapsto s \cdot n\),
532	  \(m \otimes s \mapsto s \cdot m\)); the right action map is
533	  \(a_R = \mathrm{id}_M \otimes a_N\) and the left action map is
534	  \(a_L = (a_M \otimes \mathrm{id}_N) \circ \mathrm{assoc}^{-1}\), where
535	  \(\mathrm{assoc}\) is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor associator. The projection
536	  \(\pi\) is the additive homomorphism produced by the abelian universal property
537	  \cref{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib} from the \(S\)-balanced bilinear map
538	  \((m, n) \mapsto m \otimes_S n\).
539	
540	  \emph{Cofork condition.} On an elementary tensor
541	  \(m \otimes (s \otimes n)\) we have
542	  \(\pi(a_L(m \otimes s \otimes n)) = (s \cdot m) \otimes_S n
543	  = m \otimes_S (s \cdot n) = \pi(a_R(m \otimes s \otimes n))\), the middle
544	  equality being the defining \(S\)-balancing relation of \(M \otimes_S N\); since
545	  elementary tensors generate, \(\pi \circ a_L = \pi \circ a_R\).
546	
547	  \emph{Universal property.} Given any abelian group \(P\) and a homomorphism
548	  \(g : M \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} N \to P\) with \(g \circ a_L = g \circ a_R\), the
549	  bilinear map \((m, n) \mapsto g(m \otimes n)\) is \(S\)-balanced (that is exactly
550	  the cofork condition read on elementary tensors), so by
551	  \cref{lem:tensorProduct_liftAddHom_mathlib} it factors as a unique
552	  \(\widetilde{g} : M \otimes_S N \to P\) with \(\widetilde{g} \circ \pi = g\).
553	  Existence is this descended map; uniqueness follows because \(\pi\) is
554	  surjective, hence an epimorphism of abelian groups, so \(\widetilde{g} \circ \pi
555	  = g\) determines \(\widetilde{g}\). Packaging existence and uniqueness gives the
556	  colimit cofork.
557	
558	\end{proof}
559	
560	\begin{lemma}[Relative tensor of module presheaves as a coequalizer]
561	  \label{lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}
562	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relativeTensorCoequalizerIso}
563	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
564	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
565	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
566	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}
567	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
568	  underlying abelian-group presheaf of their relative tensor product
569	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
570	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) is naturally the coequalizer of the
571	  parallel pair
572	  \[
573	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
574	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
575	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
576	  \]
577	  of abelian-group presheaves, where the first map acts the ring factor on the left,
578	  \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto (r \cdot p) \otimes q\), and the second on the
579	  right, \(p \otimes r \otimes q \mapsto p \otimes (r \cdot q)\). The identification
580	  is objectwise and natural in \(U\).
581	\end{lemma}
582	
583	\begin{proof}
584	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
585	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
586	    lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib,
587	    lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}
588	  The argument promotes the objectwise coequalizer of
589	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} to the functor category, in
590	  three steps.
591	
592	  \textbf{1. Objectwise.} For each open \(U\), apply
593	  \cref{lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer} with \(S = \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
594	  \(M = \mathcal{P}(U)\), \(N = \mathcal{Q}(U)\): the abelian group
595	  \(\mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is the coequalizer
596	  in \(\mathrm{AddCommGrp}\) of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action maps
597	  \[
598	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
599	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
600	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
601	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
602	  \]
603	  with cofork map the projection \(\pi_U\).
604	
605	  \textbf{2. Naturality / promotion.} The three objectwise families assemble into
606	  morphisms of abelian-group presheaves
607	  \[
608	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
609	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
610	    \;\overset{a_L,\,a_R}{\rightrightarrows}\;
611	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
612	    \;\overset{\pi}{\longrightarrow}\;
613	    \bigl(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\bigr),
614	  \]
615	  i.e.\ natural transformations of functors
616	  \(\mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\). Naturality of
617	  \(a_L, a_R, \pi\) in \(U\) is the compatibility of the module action with the
618	  restriction maps of \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{O}_X, \mathcal{Q}\): the restriction
619	  of \(s \cdot m\) is \((s|_V) \cdot (m|_V)\), and likewise for the projection,
620	  because the relative tensor is functorial in \(U\). Colimits in a functor
621	  category are computed objectwise --- by
622	  \cref{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib} a cocone is a colimit as
623	  soon as every evaluation \((\mathrm{ev}_U)\) of it is --- so the objectwise
624	  coequalizers of step 1 assemble into a coequalizer of the displayed parallel
625	  pair in \(\mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{AddCommGrp}\).
626	
627	  \textbf{3. Identification of the apex.} The apex presheaf
628	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\) is, by the
629	  objectwise tensor formula of the presheaf monoidal structure
630	  (\cref{lem:presheaf_tensorObj_obj_mathlib}: \((\mathcal{M}_1 \otimes_p
631	  \mathcal{M}_2)(U) = \mathcal{M}_1(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
632	  \mathcal{M}_2(U)\)), exactly the underlying abelian-group presheaf of the
633	  relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\)
634	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Transporting the colimit of step 2
635	  along this identification exhibits
636	  \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{Q}\), as an abelian-group
637	  presheaf, as the coequalizer of the parallel pair, naturally in \(U\).
638	\end{proof}
639	
640	\begin{lemma}[Objectwise colimits in a functor category]
641	  \label{lem:evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits_mathlib}
642	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits}
643	  \mathlibok
644	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
645	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.Basic}).}
646	  Let \(\mathcal{D}\) be a category and \(\mathcal{A}\) a category in which the
647	  relevant colimits exist. A cocone over a diagram
648	  \(F : \mathcal{J} \to (\mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{A})\) in the functor category is
649	  a colimit cocone as soon as, for every object \(U\) of \(\mathcal{D}\), its image
650	  under evaluation \(\mathrm{ev}_U : (\mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{A}) \to \mathcal{A}\)
651	  is a colimit cocone in \(\mathcal{A}\). Equivalently, colimits in a functor
652	  category are computed objectwise.
653	\end{lemma}

657	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj}
658	  \mathlibok

663	  \(R\). For every object \(U\) the value of their monoidal product

673	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification inverts the whiskered localization unit]
674	  \label{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
675	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
676	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
677	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
678	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
679	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer,
680	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer}
681	  Let \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) be presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules,
682	  let \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to (\mathcal{P})^{\#}\) be the unit of
683	  the sheafification adjunction
684	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}), viewed as a morphism of
685	  presheaves of modules, and write
686	  \[
687	    \eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q} :
688	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
689	    \longrightarrow (\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}
690	  \]
691	  for its right whiskering by \(\mathcal{Q}\) in the presheaf monoidal structure
692	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}). Then the image of
693	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) under the sheafification functor,
694	  \[
695	    (\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#} :
696	    (\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q})^{\#}
697	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
698	    \bigl((\mathcal{P})^{\#} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\bigr)^{\#},
699	  \]
700	  is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules. This is the strong-monoidality
701	  comparison of the module sheafification functor on a whiskered unit.
702	\end{lemma}
703	
704	\begin{proof}
705	  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
706	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib,
707	    lem:isIso_sheafification_map_iff, lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit,
708	    lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer,
709	    lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer,
710	    lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso, def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
711	  Module sheafification is a localization functor: it inverts exactly the class
712	  \(W\) of morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying morphism of
713	  abelian-group presheaves is a local isomorphism --- that is, lies in the class
714	  \(J.W\) of weak equivalences attached to the Grothendieck topology \(J\) on
715	  \(X\) (the inverse image of \(J.W\) along the forgetful functor to
716	  abelian-group presheaves). By the localization criterion for a reflective
717	  adjunction, \((f)^{\#}\) is an isomorphism if and only if \(f \in W\), i.e.\
718	  if and only if the underlying abelian-presheaf morphism of \(f\) lies in
719	  \(J.W\). It therefore suffices to prove that the underlying abelian morphism
720	  of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) lies in \(J.W\).
721	
722	  \textbf{The relative tensor as a coequalizer.} The underlying abelian-group
723	  presheaf of the relative tensor product \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\)
724	  is the objectwise relative tensor
725	  \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)} \mathcal{Q}(U)\). For each
726	  \(U\) this module is the coequalizer of the two \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-action
727	  maps
728	  \[
729	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X(U)
730	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)
731	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
732	    \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U),
733	  \]
734	  one acting on the left and one on the right factor. Colimits of abelian-group
735	  presheaves are computed objectwise, so \(\mathcal{P} \otimes_p \mathcal{Q}\),
736	  as an abelian-group presheaf, is the coequalizer of the corresponding parallel
737	  pair
738	  \[
739	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{O}_X
740	      \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
741	    \;\rightrightarrows\;
742	    \mathcal{P} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}
743	  \]
744	  of \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaves. Under this presentation the underlying
745	  abelian morphism of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) is the map of
746	  coequalizers induced by whiskering the two rows with
747	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} (-)\).
748	
749	  \textbf{Abelian sheafification preserves the coequalizer.} The abelian
750	  sheafification functor \(a\) (the associated-sheaf functor for \(J\) on
751	  abelian-group presheaves) is a left adjoint, hence preserves coequalizers; it
752	  therefore carries the parallel pair and its induced map to the analogous data
753	  of \(a\)-images. It remains to see that the two whiskered comparison maps
754	  relating the rows,
755	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) and
756	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}
757	  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), lie in \(J.W\) (equivalently, become
758	  isomorphisms after applying \(a\)).
759	
760	  \textbf{Whiskered units are stalkwise isomorphisms.} We argue this via stalks
761	  of abelian-group presheaves (\(\mathrm{TopCat.Presheaf.stalk}\), which exist
762	  for abelian presheaves on the underlying space, unlike module-sheaf stalks).
763	  The sheafification unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to a\mathcal{P}\)
764	  is a \emph{stalkwise isomorphism}: for each point \(x\) the stalk map
765	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})_x : \mathcal{P}_x \to (a\mathcal{P})_x\) is an
766	  isomorphism (the defining property of the associated-sheaf unit). The stalk of
767	  a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor of stalks,
768	  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{B})_x \cong
769	  \mathcal{A}_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{B}_x\), because the stalk is a
770	  filtered colimit and \(\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}\) commutes with filtered colimits.
771	  Under this identification the stalk of
772	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) at \(x\) is
773	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), an isomorphism
774	  (tensoring an isomorphism with the identity). Hence each whiskered unit is a
775	  stalkwise isomorphism, and a stalkwise isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves
776	  on a topological space is a local isomorphism, i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\)
777	  (\cref{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}). Applying \(a\) therefore turns
778	  both whiskered comparison maps into isomorphisms.
779	
780	  An isomorphism between two parallel-pair diagrams induces an isomorphism of
781	  their coequalizers; applied to the rows above, this shows that \(a\) sends the
782	  underlying abelian morphism of \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q}\) to an
783	  isomorphism. Hence that morphism lies in \(J.W\), so
784	  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q} \in W\), and
785	  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \rhd \mathcal{Q})^{\#}\) is an isomorphism.
786	\end{proof}

803	  \]
804	  of sheaves of modules (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}), natural in all three
805	  arguments.
806	\end{corollary}

812	  Write \(a, b, c\) for the underlying presheaves of modules of

815	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} the two iterated products unfold to
816	  \[

818	      = \bigl((a \otimes_p b)^{\#} \otimes_p c\bigr)^{\#},
819	    \qquad
820	    \mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})
821	      = \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)^{\#}\bigr)^{\#},
822	  \]
823	  each carrying an \emph{inner} sheafification that obstructs a direct appeal to
824	  the presheaf associator. The whiskered units remove these inner
825	  sheafifications. Applying \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}
826	  with \(\mathcal{P} = a \otimes_p b\) and \(\mathcal{Q} = c\), the morphism
827	  \[

829	    \bigl((a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c\bigr)^{\#}
830	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }

832	    = (\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}
833	  \]
834	  is an isomorphism; and, reading the same lemma through the braiding of the
835	  presheaf monoidal structure (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}) so as
836	  to whisker the unit \(\eta_{b \otimes_p c}\) on the left of \(a\), the
837	  morphism
838	  \[
839	    (a \lhd \eta_{b \otimes_p c})^{\#} :
840	    \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)\bigr)^{\#}
841	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
842	    \bigl(a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)^{\#}\bigr)^{\#}
843	    = \mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})
844	  \]
845	  is an isomorphism. Between the two un-sheafified middle terms sits the
846	  sheafification of the presheaf associator
847	  \[
848	    \alpha^p_{a,b,c} :
849	    (a \otimes_p b) \otimes_p c
850	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
851	    a \otimes_p (b \otimes_p c)
852	  \]
853	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), an isomorphism because
854	  sheafification is a functor and \(\alpha^p\) is one. The associator is the
855	  composite
856	  \[
857	    \alpha_{\mathcal{A},\mathcal{B},\mathcal{C}}
858	    = (a \lhd \eta_{b \otimes_p c})^{\#}
859	      \circ (\alpha^p_{a,b,c})^{\#}
860	      \circ \bigl((\eta_{a \otimes_p b} \rhd c)^{\#}\bigr)^{-1},
861	  \]
862	  an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
863	  \(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{B}, \mathcal{C}\) follows from naturality of the unit,
864	  of the whiskerings, and of the presheaf associator.

867	\begin{lemma}[Tensor-power comparison isomorphism]
868	  \label{lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
869	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd}
870	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj}
871	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", \S "Invertible modules"
872	  % (Tag 01CU, following `definition-powers') (read from
873	  % references/stacks-modules.tex, L4249-L4254).
874	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
875	  % "With this definition we have canonical isomorphisms
876	  %  $\mathcal{L}^{\otimes n} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
877	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \to
878	  %  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes n + m}$, and these isomorphisms
879	  %  satisfy a commutativity and an associativity constraint
880	  %  (formulation omitted)."
881	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Sheaves of Modules", Tag 01CU (canonical
882	  power isomorphisms).}
883	  For a line bundle \(\mathcal{L}\) and \(m, m' \in \mathbb{N}\) there is a
884	  canonical isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
885	  \[
886	    \mu_{m,m'} :
887	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
888	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m')},
889	  \]
890	  natural in \(\mathcal{L}\), and these isomorphisms satisfy a commutativity
891	  constraint (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) agrees with \(\mu_{m',m}\) after the braiding) and
892	  an associativity constraint (the two ways of combining
893	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
894	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''}\) into \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\) agree).
895	\end{lemma}

897	\begin{proof}
898	  \uses{def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafTensorObj, cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc,
899	    def:tensorObjUnitIso, def:tensorBraiding,
900	    lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit,
901	    lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
902	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
903	  We do \emph{not} build a full monoidal structure on the whole category
904	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) --- none is constructed here --- but only assemble
905	  the comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\) from those structural isomorphisms of
906	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} that the induction actually uses: the associator,
907	  the braiding, and the left unitor.
908	
909	  \textbf{The structural maps.} The braiding and the unitors descend to
910	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) directly: each is the sheafification of the
911	  corresponding isomorphism of the presheaf symmetric monoidal structure
912	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and sheafification, being a
913	  functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. The \emph{associator} is not
914	  the sheafification of a single presheaf isomorphism, because the iterated
915	  sheaf tensor products
916	  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}) \otimes \mathcal{C}\) and
917	  \(\mathcal{A} \otimes (\mathcal{B} \otimes \mathcal{C})\) each carry an inner
918	  sheafification (\cref{def:sheafTensorObj}); it is supplied instead by
919	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, whose construction clears those inner
920	  sheafifications using the strong-monoidality comparison
921	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit}. These are the only
922	  isomorphisms of sheaves of modules the construction invokes; right-exactness
923	  of the tensor product is never used.
924	
925	  \textbf{Construction of \(\mu_{m,m'}\).} Fix \(m'\) and induct on \(m\). For
926	  \(m = 0\),
927	  \[
928	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes 0} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
929	      = \mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
930	      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
931	  \]
932	  is the left unitor of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}. For the inductive step the
933	  recursive definition
934	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}\)
935	  (\cref{def:sheafTensorPow}) gives
936	  \[
937	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+1)} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
938	    = (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}) \otimes
939	      \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}
940	    \xrightarrow{\ \alpha\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
941	      (\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'})
942	    \xrightarrow{\ \mathrm{id} \otimes \beta\ } \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes
943	      (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes \mathcal{L}),
944	  \]
945	  using the associator \(\alpha\) (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and then the
946	  braiding \(\beta\) of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj} whiskered into the left
947	  factor. The inverse associator \(\alpha^{-1}\)
948	  (\cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}) and the inductive comparison \(\mu_{m,m'}\)
949	  whiskered by \(\mathcal{L}\) on the right then carry this to
950	  \[
951	    (\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'}) \otimes
952	      \mathcal{L}
953	    \xrightarrow{\ \mu_{m,m'} \rhd \mathcal{L}\ }
954	    \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m + m')} \otimes \mathcal{L}
955	    = \mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m + m') + 1)},
956	  \]
957	  the final equality being the recursive definition. The reindexing
958	  \((m + m') + 1 = (m + 1) + m'\) identifies the target with
959	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes ((m+1) + m')}\), and the composite is
960	  \(\mu_{m+1,m'}\), an isomorphism as a composite of isomorphisms. Naturality in
961	  \(\mathcal{L}\) holds because each constituent comparison is natural.
962	
963	  \textbf{Commutativity and associativity constraints.} The braiding and
964	  unitors are the sheafifications of the symmetric-monoidal coherence data of
965	  \(\PshMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}), and
966	  the associator is intertwined with the presheaf associator by the comparison
967	  isomorphisms of \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} through the
968	  construction of \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}. Both the commutativity square
969	  (\(\mu_{m,m'}\) versus \(\mu_{m',m}\) postcomposed with the braiding) and the
970	  associativity pentagon (the two bracketings of
971	  \(\mathcal{L}^{\otimes m} \otimes \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m'} \otimes
972	  \mathcal{L}^{\otimes m''} \to \mathcal{L}^{\otimes (m+m'+m'')}\)) are therefore
973	  images, under the sheafification functor together with these comparison
974	  isomorphisms, of the corresponding coherence diagrams of the presheaf
975	  symmetric monoidal structure, which commute by Mac\,Lane coherence. Since a
976	  functor preserves commuting diagrams and the comparison isomorphisms are
977	  natural, the constraints hold for the family \(\{\mu_{m,m'}\}\). We emphasise
978	  that this argument uses only the single strong-monoidality comparison
979	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit} and the resulting associator
980	  \cref{cor:sheafTensorObjAssoc}, not a full monoidal structure on
981	  \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
982	\end{proof}

984	\section{Lax-monoidal global sections}
985	\label{sec:sgr_lax_sections}
986	
987	The graded multiplication is supplied by the lax-monoidal structure of the
988	global-sections functor \(\Gamma(X, -) : \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X) \to
989	\mathrm{ModuleCat}(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X))\). It is only \emph{lax} because
990	global sections do not commute with sheafification: a global section of a
991	sheafified tensor product is not in general a finite sum of elementary tensors of
992	global sections.
993	
994	\begin{definition}\leanok
995	[Section multiplication]
996	  \label{def:sectionMul}
997	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul}
998	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
999	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
1000	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
1001	  \emph{section multiplication} is the \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-bilinear map
1002	  \[
1003	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)}
1004	      \Gamma(X, \mathcal{G})
1005	    \;\longrightarrow\;
1006	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})
1007	  \]
1008	  defined as follows. A pair of global sections \((\sigma, \tau)\) determines,
1009	  by the objectwise formula of \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} at the
1010	  top open, the elementary tensor \(\sigma \otimes \tau \in
1011	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})(X)\), a global section of
1012	  the tensor product presheaf. Composing with the global-sections component of
1013	  the sheafification unit
1014	  \(\eta : \mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G} \to
1015	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})^{\#} =
1016	  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\)
1017	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib},
1018	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}) yields a global section
1019	  \(\sigma \cdot \tau \in
1020	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})\). Bilinearity over
1021	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) is inherited from bilinearity of the elementary
1022	  tensor and \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-linearity of \(\eta\).
1023	\end{definition}
1024	
1025	\begin{lemma}[Coherence of the section multiplication]
1026	  \label{lem:sectionMul_coherent}
1027	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul_assoc_unit}
1028	  \uses{def:sectionMul, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib, lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
1029	  The section multiplications (\cref{def:sectionMul}) are associative and unital
1030	  in the following sense. For sheaves \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}, \mathcal{H}\)

/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor triple presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ (𝒪_X(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q))` of two
presheaves of modules over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the
`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring
restriction map of `𝒪_X`).  This is the **domain** row of the relative-tensor coequalizer
presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which
the two `R(U)`-action maps `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`/`actRmap` act.  Project-local: no
objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib. -/
noncomputable def relTensorTriplePresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U))
  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
    (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
      (TensorProduct.map (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
        (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap)).toAddMonoidHom
  map_id U := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext; ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n => simp; rfl
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext; ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n => simp; rfl
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aL`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P` factor).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aL (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext; ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp only [map_zero]
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_zero, map_zero]
      | tmul s n => sorry
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.aL`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P` factor).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        simp [RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap, RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actM,
          PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      | add a b ha hb => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
input.new_string:
/-- Pure-`LinearMap` naturality square for the left action map: restriction commutes with
`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`, the content being `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P`
factor.  Stated at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps to sidestep the `AddCommGrpCat` coercion layer; the
natural-transformation `relTensorActL` transports it through `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. -/
private lemma relTensorActL_naturality_linear (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj V) (P.obj V) (Q.obj V)).comp
        (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
          (TensorProduct.map (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap))
      = (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap).comp
          (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)) := by
  apply TensorProduct.ext'
  intro m y
  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero => simp
  | tmul s n =>
    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, TensorProduct.map_tmul, RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul,
      LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq, PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
  | add a b ha hb => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P` factor).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom]
    ext x
    simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (relTensorActL_naturality_linear P Q f) x

507	natural-transformation `relTensorActL` transports it through `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. -/
508	private lemma relTensorActL_naturality_linear (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules)
509	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
510	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj V) (P.obj V) (Q.obj V)).comp
511	        (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
512	          (TensorProduct.map (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
513	            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap))
514	      = (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
515	            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap).comp

517	  apply TensorProduct.ext'
518	  intro m y
519	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
520	  | zero => simp
521	  | tmul s n =>

523	      RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul,
524	      TensorProduct.map_tmul, PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
525	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]

527	/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
528	whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
529	`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
530	restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P` factor).  This is the first of the two
531	parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`). -/
532	noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
533	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where

541	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]

/-- Pure-`LinearMap` naturality square for the left action map: restriction commutes with
`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`, the content being `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P`
factor.  Stated at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps to sidestep the `AddCommGrpCat` coercion layer; the
natural-transformation `relTensorActL` transports it through `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. -/
private lemma relTensorActL_naturality_linear (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules)
    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj V) (P.obj V) (Q.obj V)).comp
        (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
          (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap))
      = (TensorProduct.map (P.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap
            (Q.presheaf.map f).hom.toIntLinearMap).comp
          (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)) := by
  apply TensorProduct.ext'
  intro m y
  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero => simp
  | tmul s n =>
    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, TensorProduct.map_tmul, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
      RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul]
    erw [LinearMap.comp_apply, RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
      PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
  | add a b ha hb => simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_add, map_add, ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the `P` factor).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom]
    ext x
    simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (relTensorActL_naturality_linear P Q f) x

input.new_string:
/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged onto the abelian restriction
`P.presheaf.map f` by `PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).
The `P.obj`-carrier ascriptions on the restriction maps of `relTensorTriplePresheaf`/
`relTensorDomainPresheaf` keep the carriers syntactically aligned with `actLmap` so the naturality
reduction goes through. -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply]
        simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
          LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
          RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe,
          PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      | add a b ha hb => simp [TensorProduct.tmul_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp [ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
whose component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is the compatibility of the module action with the
restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged onto the abelian restriction
`P.presheaf.map f` by `PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`).  This is the first of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).
The `P.obj`-carrier ascriptions on the restriction maps of `relTensorTriplePresheaf`/
`relTensorDomainPresheaf` keep the carriers syntactically aligned with `actLmap` so the naturality
reduction goes through. -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]

      | zero => simp

          PresheafOfModules.map_smul]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
component at `U` the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged onto the abelian
restriction `P.presheaf.map f` by `PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`.  First of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).

The component carries `actLmap` over the `(P.presheaf).obj U`-carriers (with the `Module` instances
transported by `inferInstanceAs` from the `P.obj U` instances; these carriers are `rfl`-defeq) so
that the action map's domain matches the restriction maps' codomain SYNTACTICALLY — otherwise the
naturality reduction stalls under the diamond (`simp`'s congruence motive cannot descend through the
`(P.presheaf).obj`-vs-`P.obj` defeq gap). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U :=
    letI : Module ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ↥((P.presheaf).obj U) := inferInstanceAs (Module _ ↥(P.obj U))
    letI : Module ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ↥((Q.presheaf).obj U) := inferInstanceAs (Module _ ↥(Q.obj U))
    AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
      (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ((P.presheaf).obj U) ((Q.presheaf).obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply]
        simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
          LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
          RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe,
          PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      | add a b ha hb => simp [TensorProduct.tmul_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp [ha, hb]

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
component at `U` the objectwise left-action map `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`,
`m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged onto the abelian
restriction `P.presheaf.map f` by `PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`.  First of the two
parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).

The component carries `actLmap` over the `(P.presheaf).obj U`-carriers (with the `Module` instances
transported by `inferInstanceAs` from the `P.obj U` instances; these carriers are `rfl`-defeq) so
that the action map's domain matches the restriction maps' codomain SYNTACTICALLY — otherwise the
naturality reduction stalls under the diamond (`simp`'s congruence motive cannot descend through the
`(P.presheaf).obj`-vs-`P.obj` defeq gap). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U :=
    letI : Module ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ↥((P.presheaf).obj U) := inferInstanceAs (Module _ ↥(P.obj U))
    letI : Module ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ↥((Q.presheaf).obj U) := inferInstanceAs (Module _ ↥(Q.obj U))
    AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
      (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) ((P.presheaf).obj U) ((Q.presheaf).obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply]
        simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
          LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
          RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe,
          PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      | add a b ha hb => simp [TensorProduct.tmul_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp [ha, hb]

  leaf and `simp only [TensorProduct.tmul_zero/​tmul_add, map_zero/​map_add]` at the others.
- **Carrier choice:** the middle ring factor is `X.sheaf.obj.obj U` (CommRingCat), NOT
  `X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj U` (RingCat). This is deliberate: `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`
  requires `[CommRing S]`, found only on `X.sheaf.obj.obj U` (verified `CommRing ↥(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj U)`
  / `CommRing ↥((X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ …).obj U)` both FAIL `inferInstance`). The two are `rfl`-defeq.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified via

/-- The **left action** natural transformation `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`,
component at `U` the objectwise `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.
First of the two parallel rows of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation
(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Naturality is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (on the `P`
factor), bridged onto the abelian restriction `P.presheaf.map f` by
`PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`.  The `tmul.tmul` leaf reduces the inner map-applications
`in conv focus` (so `simp` need not descend under the folded `actLmap` head, which otherwise stalls). -/
noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
  naturality U V f := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp
    | tmul m y =>
      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
      | zero => simp
      | tmul s n =>
        simp only [AddMonoidHom.comp_apply]
        conv_lhs => enter [2]; simp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
          LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul]
        conv_rhs => enter [2]; simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,
          RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul]
        simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,
          RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap_tmul, relTensorDomainPresheaf, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
          PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      | add a b ha hb => simp [TensorProduct.tmul_add, ha, hb]
    | add a b ha hb => simp [ha, hb]

(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Naturality is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (on the `P`
factor), bridged onto the abelian restriction `P.presheaf.map f` by
`PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe`.  The `tmul.tmul` leaf reduces the inner map-applications
`in conv focus` (so `simp` need not descend under the folded `actLmap` head, which otherwise stalls). -/

    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap
    (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom

    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
    ext x
    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
    | zero => simp

        conv_rhs => enter [2]; simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,

full `simp`/`erw`/`rw` chains; `conv … => enter [2]` (focuses subterm but `map_tmul` still won't match
